Sydney · Built for every driver

The last mile
is broken.

Every driver wastes minutes every week circling blocks, guessing parking rules, and walking the wrong way from the car. We're fixing the part of navigation no one talks about — starting with Sydney's delivery fleets and service providers, built for everyone behind the wheel.

Driving → Parking → Walking → Arrived!

Driving → Parking → Walking → Arrived!

The problem

01
Navigation apps stop at the kerb

Every navigation app gets you to the street. None of them tell you where to actually stop, whether it's legal, or how far you'll be walking to the door.

02
Parking rules are invisible

Loading zones, clearways, timed spots — the rules change by hour, by day, by lane. Drivers guess. Drivers get fined. It eats into every delivery margin.

03
It's not just deliveries

Taking your parent to the doctor's. Picking the kids up. A first date you can't be late for. The same five minutes circling for parking happens in every part of life, not just on the job.

04
No one owns this space

The gap between "you have arrived" and actually arriving is completely unaddressed by any major navigation product. It's a blind spot hiding in plain sight.

Our answer

Navigation that finishes the job.

Parkle sequences the full last-mile flow — drive, park, walk — in a single app built for anyone who drives in the city.

We surface legal parking nearby, calculate walking time to the door, and hand off seamlessly between each phase. No switching apps. No guessing.

Need an accessible spot, an EV charger, a car share bay, or know which loading zones to avoid? Parkle filters for exactly what you need, right when you need it.

🚐

See it in action
